noveltyJustification: This iteration located an already-rich DOM geometry plane, exposed the missing screenshot artifact/publication contract, separated perceptual dedupe from semantic retrieval, and narrowed Rust's only plausible visual role to measured multimodal model execution.
focus: Pressure-test screenshot palette and spacing extraction, perceptual hashing, layout fingerprints, and multimodal embeddings; classify which need Rust versus existing JS/native tools.
answeredQuestions: Which new visual/style-analysis features are practical?
Can visual features reuse the resident model-service shape?

ruledOut.approach: Screenshot-first spacing extraction for live pages
ruledOut.reason: The existing crawler already captures DOM rectangles and computed spacing exactly.
Native-backed Node image tooling provides the initial decode and statistics baseline.

ruledOut.approach: Perceptual hash as semantic style rank
ruledOut.reason: Perceptual distance measures near-duplicate visual content rather than design-language similarity.

ruledOut.approach: Reuse the text embedding ABI unchanged for images
ruledOut.reason: Image modality requires distinct preprocessing, profile identity, payload, and cache semantics.

ruledOut.approach: Monolithic Rust visual-analysis worker before measurement
ruledOut.reason: It couples independent features and release boundaries without proven compute residency.

graphEvents.label: Perceptual hash serves dedupe and drift
Multimodal retrieval needs a new ABI
Rust is conditional for model execution only
Design crawler screenshots and element styles

label: DOM geometry makes spacing and layout fingerprints a TypeScript crawler feature
Screenshot-derived features require a versioned artifact and generation-publication contract
Perceptual hashing is useful for dedupe and drift but not semantic style ranking
Multimodal retrieval is new capability but requires a modality-aware ABI and profile
Only measured multimodal model execution is a plausible Rust visual kernel
TypeScript owns capture, viewport policy, hashes, schema publication, fusion, fallback, and telemetry
Crawler already captures five screenshots plus rich DOM geometry and computed styles
Styles artifact allowlist has no screenshot or derived visual-feature role

Multimodal retrieval needs a new ABI

direction: Screenshot-first spacing extraction for live pages
reason: DOM rectangles and computed styles are already exact and available in the crawler.
Native-backed Node image tooling supplies the initial baseline.

direction: Perceptual hash as semantic style rank
reason: Near-duplicate pixel distance is not design-language similarity.

direction: Reuse the text embedding ABI unchanged for images
reason: Image profiles require modality-specific payloads, preprocessing, identity, and caches.

direction: Monolithic Rust visual-analysis worker before measurement
reason: It couples independent features without proven compute residency.
